title: GPU-Accelerated Boids Flocking Simulation
prompt:
Use Three.js to implement a GPU-accelerated Boids flocking simulation based on DataTexture, with 500 particles following separation-alignment-cohesion rules.

[Three.js Import (must follow exactly)]
Place the following importmap in <head> and import via ES Module in <script type="module">:
<script type="importmap">
{ "imports": {
    "three": "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/three@0.170.0/build/three.module.js",
    "three/addons/": "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/three@0.170.0/examples/jsm/"
} }
</script>
<script type="module">
import * as THREE from 'three';
import { OrbitControls } from 'three/addons/controls/OrbitControls.js';
import { GPUComputationRenderer } from 'three/addons/misc/GPUComputationRenderer.js';
// Your code here
</script>

[GPGPU Architecture]
(1) Use GPUComputationRenderer (from three/addons/misc/GPUComputationRenderer.js) to implement GPU-side Boids simulation.
(2) Two DataTextures (size width=32, height=16 -> 512 texels, first 500 texels store boid data):
  - texturePosition: RGBA -> (x, y, z, unused or heading)
  - textureVelocity: RGBA -> (vx, vy, vz, unused or speed)
(3) Position initialization: 500 boids randomly distributed within a [-25, 25]^3 cube.
(4) Velocity initialization: random directions, speed 1-3 units/s.

[Boids Rules - GPU Shaders]
(5) Implement classic Boids three rules in the velocity compute shader:
  - Separation: maintain distance from neighbors within separationRadius, generating repulsion force.
  - Alignment: align velocity direction with neighbors within alignmentRadius.
  - Cohesion: move toward the centroid of neighbors within alignmentRadius, strength controlled by cohesion parameter.
(6) New velocity = old velocity + separation * separationWeight + alignment * alignmentWeight + cohesion * cohesionWeight.
(7) Speed clamping: normalize then multiply by speedLimit (if speed exceeds speedLimit).

[Boundary Handling]
(8) Boids operate within a [-25, 25]^3 cube space.
(9) When a boid approaches a boundary (distance < 5 units), apply a bounce-back force toward center (proportional to penetration depth).

[Predator Effect]
(10) When mouse moves on the canvas, use Raycaster to project mouse position onto the y=0 plane to get a 3D position.
(11) Pass this position as a predator uniform to the compute shader.
(12) Each boid within 10 units of the predator generates an escape force (direction away from predator, strength inversely proportional to distance).

[Rendering]
(13) 500 boids rendered using InstancedMesh (ConeGeometry as boid shape, height=0.5, radius=0.15).
(14) Each frame, read back position/velocity data from DataTexture (use renderer.readRenderTargetPixels or read from texture.image.data on the JS side), update each instance matrix:
  - Position from texturePosition
  - Orientation from textureVelocity (cone tip points in velocity direction, using quaternion.setFromUnitVectors)
(15) Boid color: map to color temperature based on speed magnitude (slow=blue, fast=red) or use uniform bright color.

[Visual Aids]
(16) Bounding box: LineSegments drawing the [-25, 25]^3 cube wireframe (white, semi-transparent).
(17) Optional: boid trails or motion blur (enhances visuals but not required).

[Control Panel]
(18) Separation radius slider id="separationRadius", range 1-10, step 0.5, default 3.
(19) Alignment radius slider id="alignmentRadius", range 5-20, step 1, default 10.
(20) Cohesion strength slider id="cohesion", range 0-2, step 0.1, default 1.0.
(21) Speed limit slider id="speedLimit", range 1-20, step 1, default 8.

[State Exposure (required)]
(22) Update window.__3D_STATE__ every frame (or every 10 frames), including:
  - boidCount: number (500)
  - separationRadius: number
  - alignmentRadius: number
  - cohesion: number
  - speedLimit: number
  - avgSpeed: number (average speed scalar of all boids)
  - avgNeighborDist: number (average distance of randomly sampled 50 boid pairs, or another reasonable clustering metric)
  - centerOfMass: { x: number, y: number, z: number } (centroid of all 500 boids)

Output a single HTML file that runs directly in the browser.
difficulty_analysis: L5: (1) GPUComputationRenderer DataTexture initialization and compute shader pipeline; (2) Implementing Boids three rules in GLSL requires iterating over all neighbors (O(n^2) parallelized on GPU); (3) Boundary bounce-back and predator escape forces; (4) GPU-to-CPU data readback and InstancedMesh matrix updates; (5) Quaternion calculation for orientation; (6) Statistical computation of avgSpeed/avgNeighborDist/centerOfMass
